items.reason: All 20 areas listed in `docs/decisions/decisions-needed.md` map to decisions DEC-001 through DEC-020 in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md`, each with id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences (with concrete 'removes' bullets), and non-empty projects_to lists pointing to specific dictionary, migration, function, or test artifacts.

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` contains two entries (PEND-001-runtime-slice-schedule, PEND-005-deno-check-in-ci) with owner (Andy), target dates (2026-05-15, 2026-05-03) that are not past relative to evidence date 2026-04-26, blockers, and one-line rationales.

Each decision's consequences names concrete artifacts that are removed or collapsed. E.g., DEC-001 'Removes per-function subject-to-person merge branches from supabase/functions/lti-launch-handler/index.ts'; DEC-007 'Removes duplicate source-ID columns from every public table'; DEC-014 justifies preserving static mirrors against deletion by naming offline portal review as the cost. Decisions that preserve multiple surfaces (DEC-002, DEC-005, DEC-009, DEC-014) include explicit 'Why not collapse' justifications.

Each decision lists three credible options bracketing the design space. E.g., DEC-001 considers separate-table-per-spec (permissive), full collapse into person (aggressive), and the chosen person+agent split. DEC-005 brackets AGS-as-canonical (aggressive), peer source-native stores (permissive), and the current-state+history middle. DEC-014 brackets full deletion of static mirrors, treating them as canonical contracts, and keeping them as documentation fixtures. The aggressive options are real architectural choices, not strawmen.

`scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` reads `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` and projects to `dictionary/*.v1.json`; the seed's `projection_summary` shows 5 projections covering 59 objects and 575 fields. VERIFY.md runs `--check` mode, and the script implements drift detection that fails CI if per-spec files diverge. Hand-editing per-spec files is documented as a build error in DEC-016.

The seed's `canonical_objects` array carries `canonical.identity.person.email` with projections to `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.email`, `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor.email`, `dictionary/qti-core.v1.json#qti_candidate.email`, and `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership.email` — the four fields the rubric explicitly names. Each per-spec field carries `canonical_field_id`. DEC-017 governs the rule.

The seed defines `canonical.identity.person` as a real canonical object with fields (id, display_name, email, source_subject_id, identity_kind), allowed values for identity_kind, and explicit projections to OneRoster Person, Caliper actor, QTI candidate, LTI membership, and LTI launch subject. The unification is data, not just prose in DEC-001.

Per-spec dictionaries reference `shared_enum_id` (e.g., role_family, organization_type, action, grading_progress) and the shared enums carry both `crosswalk` (spec-native → canonical) and `reverse_crosswalk` (canonical → all spec-native values) arrays. The grading_progress enum in `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json` shows OneRoster fullyGraded and LTI FullyGraded both mapping to canonical fully_graded with the reverse direction enumerated. `check_dictionary_artifacts.py#check_shared_enum` enforces this structure.

DEC-019 closes the privacy-class vocabulary at 9 values (public, operational, directory, education_record, behavioral, sensitive, credential, privacy_governance, system) and explicitly invalidates `depends_on_entity`/`depends_on_contents`. `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py#validate_privacy_class` rejects those placeholders and any value outside the closed list, blocking generation. Caliper extension fields use `privacy_classification`/`allowed_by_policy` resolutions per occurrence rather than placeholders.

The seed's `migration_generation` block declares 13 relationship_keys (organization_parent, course_org, class_course, etc.) with FK targets, cardinalities, ownership, and delete behavior. `scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py` reads that graph and emits `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql`; VERIFY.md runs `--check` mode to fail on drift. DEC-020 governs the direction.

Per-spec dictionary fields carry `decision_id` and `check_dictionary_artifacts.py` enforces a decision-trace round-trip: it parses the JSON block in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` between the decision-trace markers and fails if any field references an unknown decision_id or is missing from the matching produces_fields list. Shared enum entries carry decision_id 'DEC-018-global-enum-crosswalks'.

Generators `generate_oneroster_core.py`, `generate_qti_core.py`, `generate_case_core.py`, `generate_caliper_core.py`, and `generate_integration_governance_core.py` each emit Markdown, HTML, SQL comments, and OpenAPI from their structured source. `check_dictionary_artifacts.py` verifies every object/field/value/decision_id appears in all four artifact types, and VERIFY.md re-runs the generators each iteration. The build guide and other prose link to dictionary anchors (e.g., `oneroster-core-dictionary.html#person.email`).

`docs/lead-spec-accounting.md` includes a 'Decision Citation Map' table giving a one-line plain-language explanation of what each cited decision fixes (e.g., DEC-010 'Tenant-owned roster and gradebook records are isolated by RLS; shared reference data is separate'). `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` cites DEC-010 inline at the runtime tenant_id step. The decision register itself renders as `site/docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html` with per-decision anchors and plain-language consequences for each, reachable from the portal per the link-check report.

`docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` walks all five required steps: school + 1 teacher + 10 students (Section 1), class with all enrollments (Section 2), numeric grade on a line item (Section 3), CASE URI attached via `line_items.case_target_uri` (Section 4), and a Caliper-style class activity feed read via `class_activity_feed` (Section 5). Every step has copy-pasteable curl against `https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co/rest/v1/...` with full JSON payloads. Each field and enum value referenced is linked to its dictionary anchor (e.g., `oneroster-core-dictionary.html#enum.role_family.teacher`).

`site/api/site-link-check.json` reports status 'pass' with 0 errors across 202 local links over 36 HTML pages, 634 dictionary entries checked, 20 decisions checked, and 12 API endpoints checked. All 36 HTML pages are reachable from `site/index.html` within 3 clicks; the buildability guide is reachable in 1 click. `scripts/check_site_links.py` runs in VERIFY.md.

Reading only `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` (and the dictionary/decision pages it links to), a layperson can copy-paste executable curl for every step of the fixed scenario. Section 0 explains how to obtain the service-role key from the Supabase dashboard (link to `supabase-hosted-database.html#where-to-find-the-service-role-key`); subsequent sections use a tenant-scoped user JWT and the publishable key (already in `.env.example`). The runtime tables and views (`organizations`, `people`, `enrollments`, `line_items`, `results`, `caliper_events`, `class_activity_feed`) are present in the generated Supabase migration, and the live tests `supabase_tenant_rls_test.py` and `supabase_audit_log_test.py` exercise the same auth + RLS path the guide uses.

`scripts/evaluate_platform.py` defines `ensure_traced_causes`, `normalize_traced_cause`, and `DEFAULT_TRACED_CAUSES` so every non-pass verdict gets a non-empty `traced_cause` naming the lowest-layer artifact and required change. `normalize_buildability_gaps` enforces a `traced_cause` on every gap. The script also auto-blocks runtime items via `apply_prereq_blocks` when external prerequisites are missing.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, which runs after every iteration. The script writes per-item status, traced_cause for non-pass items, and a buildability_gaps array, plus counts and a `done` flag.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` defines `OVERRIDES_PATH = docs/loop-overrides.md`, calls `read_overrides(repo)` at the top of each iteration before running Codex, halts via `overrides_paused()` when a `## Pause` section contains a bare `PAUSE` token (with HTML comments stripped), and `build_prompt` injects the overrides under a 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE...)' section in the iteration prompt.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` checks `after_eval.get('done')` after each iteration and exits 0 when the LLM evaluator reports done. Numeric advisory score is logged but does not gate termination. Hard caps (`max_iterations`, `stall_limit`) remain as safety rails per the rubric.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. The teaching-app runtime also backs the narrow CASE alignment path needed by gradebook line items (`line_items.case_target_uri`) and the narrow Caliper class-feed path needed by the buildability guide (`caliper_events` plus `class_activity_feed`). QTI and full CASE import/search remain doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated table, view, Edge Function receipt, or governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, full Sensor API ingestion/projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.
